'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|公卿補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:11]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:11]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:11]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:11]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:11]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 2]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:qq]] revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:qq]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:qq]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|新撰姓氏録" to "|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku"
revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:qq]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:qq]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyotowake]][[Category:qq]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] on (CatAlot)
revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]]
