'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} [[Category:Miyotowake]][[Category:Completion tier revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Miyotowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Miyotowake]] [[ja:御友別]] [[en:Miyotowake]]

The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Miyotowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Miyotowake]] [[ja:御友別]] [[en:Miyotowake]]

'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=draft}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=draft}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Miyotowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Miyotowake]] [[ja:御友別]] [[en:Miyotowake]]

[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=draft}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=draft}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Miyotowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Miyotowake]] [[ja:御友別]] [[en:Miyotowake]]

'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake

During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. revision.text:
'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft|12=draft}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=draft|12=draft}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=draft|12=draft}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft|12=draft}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Miyotowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Miyotowake]] [[ja:御友別]] [[en:Miyotowake]]

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:御友別 up to rev 103132172
revision.text:
'''御友別'''（みともわけ{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}、御友別命）または'''吉備 御友別'''（きび の みともわけ{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[吉備氏|吉備臣（吉備氏）]]の遠祖{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 == 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]3年（[[879年]]）10月22日条では、[[吉備武彦|吉備武彦命]]の第二男とする{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』右京皇別 吉備臣条では、御友別を[[稚武彦命]]の孫とする{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+『[[日本書紀]]』応神天皇22年9月条、『[[日本三代実録]]』元慶3年10月条に基づく関係系図 |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01=[[吉備武彦]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=浦凝別
［苑臣祖］
（苑県に封）|02='''御友別'''
［吉備臣祖］|03=[[鴨別]]
［笠臣祖］
（波区芸県に封）|04={{color|#FC4E6B|兄媛}}
（[[応神天皇]]妃）
（織部を賜う）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=稲速別
［下道臣祖］
（川島県に封）|02=仲彦
［上道臣・香屋臣祖］
（上道県に封）|03=弟彦
［三野臣祖］
（三野県に封）}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[応神天皇]]22年3月14日条では、天皇妃の兄媛（えひめ）を吉備に帰郷させたとあるが、同条ではこの兄媛を吉備臣祖の御友別の妹と記している{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 同書応神天皇22年9月6日条では、天皇が吉備の葉田葦守宮（岡山市足守付近か{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}）に行幸した際、御友別は兄弟子孫を膳夫として奉仕させた。その功により、天皇は[[吉備国]]を割いて御友別子孫を次のように封じた分封比定地の記載は「吉備武彦」『日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版』 吉川弘文館、2010年による。。 * 長子の稲速別 - 川島県に封ず。下道臣祖。川島県はのちの備中国浅口郡に比定。 * 中子の仲彦 - 上道県に封ず。上道臣祖・ 香屋臣祖。上道県はのちの備前国上道郡に比定。 * 弟彦 - 三野県に封ず。三野臣祖。三野県はのちの備前国御野郡に比定。 * 弟の[[鴨別]] - 波区芸県に封ず。笠臣祖。波区芸県はのちの比定地未詳（笠岡市付近か）。 * 兄の浦凝別 - 苑県に封ず。苑臣祖。苑県はのちの備中国下道郡曾能郷か。 * 兄媛 - 織部を賜う。 そして、こうした縁で彼らの子孫は今も吉備国にいると記している{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔 == === 人物 === 文献に見える御友別の子孫として、『[[公卿補任]]』では[[吉備真備]]が九世孫と記されている{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}。 また『日本三代実録』元慶3年（879年）10月22日条によると、播磨の印南野臣の祖の人上（馬養人上/印南野人上）が御友別命十一世孫であったといい、子孫が「笠朝臣」の改賜姓を得ている{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}。 === 氏族 === 前述のように、『[[日本書紀]]』では御友別を[[吉備氏|吉備臣]]の祖とする。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 吉備臣 - 稚武彦命孫の御友別命の後。 == 考証 == 『日本書紀』では上記のように御友別の時の吉備の分封を記すが、『古事記』では[[仁徳天皇]]と黒日売（吉備海部直の女）の伝承が見えることもあり、分封伝承の成立は7世紀まで下るとする説もある{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}。 [[岡山県]][[岡山市]]の巨大古墳・[[造山古墳]]との関連性が検討されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=石井英雄|year=|chapter=吉備御友別|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=御友別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{デフォルトソート:みともわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:吉備氏]]

'''御友別'''（みともわけ{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}、御友別命）または'''吉備 御友別'''（きび の みともわけ{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[吉備氏|吉備臣（吉備氏）]]の遠祖{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 == 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]3年（[[879年]]）10月22日条では、[[吉備武彦|吉備武彦命]]の第二男とする{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』右京皇別 吉備臣条では、御友別を[[稚武彦命]]の孫とする{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+『[[日本書紀]]』応神天皇22年9月条、『[[日本三代実録]]』元慶3年10月条に基づく関係系図 |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01=[[吉備武彦]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=浦凝別
［苑臣祖］
（苑県に封）|02='''御友別'''
［吉備臣祖］|03=[[鴨別]]
［笠臣祖］
（波区芸県に封）|04={{color|#FC4E6B|兄媛}}
（[[応神天皇]]妃）
（織部を賜う）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=稲速別
［下道臣祖］
（川島県に封）|02=仲彦
［上道臣・香屋臣祖］
（上道県に封）|03=弟彦
［三野臣祖］
（三野県に封）}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[応神天皇]]22年3月14日条では、天皇妃の兄媛（えひめ）を吉備に帰郷させたとあるが、同条ではこの兄媛を吉備臣祖の御友別の妹と記している{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 同書応神天皇22年9月6日条では、天皇が吉備の葉田葦守宮（岡山市足守付近か{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}）に行幸した際、御友別は兄弟子孫を膳夫として奉仕させた。その功により、天皇は[[吉備国]]を割いて御友別子孫を次のように封じた分封比定地の記載は「吉備武彦」『日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版』 吉川弘文館、2010年による。。 * 長子の稲速別 - 川島県に封ず。下道臣祖。川島県はのちの備中国浅口郡に比定。 * 中子の仲彦 - 上道県に封ず。上道臣祖・ 香屋臣祖。上道県はのちの備前国上道郡に比定。 * 弟彦 - 三野県に封ず。三野臣祖。三野県はのちの備前国御野郡に比定。 * 弟の[[鴨別]] - 波区芸県に封ず。笠臣祖。波区芸県はのちの比定地未詳（笠岡市付近か）。 * 兄の浦凝別 - 苑県に封ず。苑臣祖。苑県はのちの備中国下道郡曾能郷か。 * 兄媛 - 織部を賜う。 そして、こうした縁で彼らの子孫は今も吉備国にいると記している{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔 == === 人物 === 文献に見える御友別の子孫として、『[[公卿補任]]』では[[吉備真備]]が九世孫と記されている{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}。 また『日本三代実録』元慶3年（879年）10月22日条によると、播磨の印南野臣の祖の人上（馬養人上/印南野人上）が御友別命十一世孫であったといい、子孫が「笠朝臣」の改賜姓を得ている{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}。 === 氏族 === 前述のように、『[[日本書紀]]』では御友別を[[吉備氏|吉備臣]]の祖とする。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 吉備臣 - 稚武彦命孫の御友別命の後。 == 考証 == 『日本書紀』では上記のように御友別の時の吉備の分封を記すが、『古事記』では[[仁徳天皇]]と黒日売（吉備海部直の女）の伝承が見えることもあり、分封伝承の成立は7世紀まで下るとする説もある{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}}。 [[岡山県]][[岡山市]]の巨大古墳・[[造山古墳]]との関連性が検討されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=石井英雄|year=|chapter=吉備御友別|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=御友別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{デフォルトソート:みともわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:吉備氏]]

'''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kuge Nenjū Gyōji|ja|Kugyō補任}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Miyotowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Miyotowake]] [[ja:御友別]] [[en:Miyotowake]] {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
[[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|qid=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|qq=|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}}

(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}}

[[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|WD=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|WD=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake

[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|WD=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|WD=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}}

[[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|WD=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake

[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]

(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
