(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}}

He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] }} [[ja:御友別]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}}

In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:御友別]] [[ja:御友別]] [[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|WD=Q18226257}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18235575]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] mentioned in texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. He is considered an ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]].{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
[Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi]
(Given Sonokori) |02='''Mitomowake'''
[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:qq]]

[Ancestor of the Kibi no Omi]|03={{ill|Kamowake|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake|1=Kamowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi]
(Given Natsukage)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Ani-hime}}
(Consort of Emperor Ōjin)
(Received Orihime)} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahayawake
[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko

(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|en|Kibi clan|WD=Q11412645|lt=Kibi clan|1=Kibi clan}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake

[Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi]
(Given Kawashima) |02=Nakahiko
[Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi]
(Given Kamitsudo)|03=Ototo-hiko
[Ancestor of the Mino no Omi]
(Given Mino)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, entry for the 22nd year, 3rd month, 14th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, states that Ani-hime, consort of the emperor, was allowed to return to Kibi. It also identifies her as the sister of Mitomowake, ancestor of the Kibi no Omi.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} On the 22nd year, 9th month, 6th day of Emperor Ōjin's reign, the emperor visited the Yoshita Ashimorimiya Palace (believed to be near {{ill|Ashimori, Okayama|ja|岡山市足守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashimori, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this visit, Mitomowake had his descendants serve as stewards. For this service, the emperor divided the Kibi Province among Mitomowake’s descendants as follows:{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} * Eldest son, Inahayawake - Given Kawashima. Ancestor of the Shimotsudo no Omi. Kawashima later became Asakuchi District, Bitchū Province. * Second son, Nakahiko - Given Kamitsudo. Ancestor of the Kamitsudo no Omi and Kaya no Omi. Kamitsudo later became Kamitsudo District, Bizen Province. * Ototo-hiko - Given Mino. Ancestor of the Mino no Omi. Mino later became Mino District, Bizen Province. * Younger brother, Kamowake - Given Natsukage. Ancestor of the Kasuga no Omi. The exact location of Natsukage is unknown, possibly near Kasukabe City. * Elder brother, Urakobiwake - Given Sonokori. Ancestor of the Sonon no Omi. Sonokori later became Sonokori in Bitchū Province. * Sister, Ani-hime - Received Orihime. These descendants are noted to remain in Kibi Province.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Descendants == === Individuals === The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} as a ninth-generation descendant of Mitomowake.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Additionally, the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), records that Jinami of the Harima-based Innami no Omi clan was an 11th-generation descendant of Mitomowake. Jinami's lineage received the surname "Kasuga Ason."{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} === Clans === As stated, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} describes Mitomowake as the ancestor of the Kibi no Omi. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clan is noted as his descendants: * Right Capital, Imperial Lineage, Kibi no Omi - Descendants of Mitomowake, grandson of Wakatakehiko. == Interpretations == While the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} describes the division of Kibi under Mitomowake, some scholars suggest that these traditions were formulated as late as the 7th century, citing the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} story of Emperor Nintoku and Kurohibime, daughter of the Kibi no Ama clan.{{Sfn|吉備御友別（国史）}} Connections with the massive {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)}} in Okayama City have also been examined. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=石井英雄|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{Harvid|吉備御友別（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588|chapter=御友別|ref={{Harvid|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} {{translated page|ja|御友別|version=103132172|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御友別]] {{wikidata link|Q18235575}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:御友別]] [[ja:御友別]] [[ja:御友別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|qid=Q18226257|1=Kibi no Takehiko}}}} '''Mitomowake''' (御友別), also known as '''Kibi no Mitomowake''' (吉備 御友別), is an ancient figure of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} mentioned in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|en|Kibi clan|qid=Q11412645|lt=Kibi clan|1=Kibi clan}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} == Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, entry for the 22nd day of the 10th month of Genkei 3 (879), identifies Mitomowake as the second son of {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, entry for the Kibi no Omi lineage in the Imperial branch of the Right Capital, describes Mitomowake as the grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}}.{{Sfn|御友別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Relationships based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Ōjin section, 22nd year of reign, and {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} Genkei 3 (879) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01={{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=Urakobiwake
